Checklists
Two tools to run your show - pick where you want to work
Production Checklist
The big picture. High-level milestones that track your show's progress from rights to strike - one check at a time. Designed for org-level visibility of where things stand.
Open checklist →
Producer To-Do
The day-to-day work. Execution tasks mapped to your milestones - what needs doing, who's on it, and what's blocked. Your producer's running list for getting the show up.
Open to-do list →
Set up your Production Checklist
A high-level checklist of every major milestone - from rights to strike. Check them off as your show moves forward.
Recommended
Ultimate Production Checklist
22 milestones and 200+ linked tasks, ready to go.
Build My Own
Start blank. Add the milestones that fit your show.
Import
Bring in a checklist you already have.

Audition Builder

ARE YOU READY?

Yay! You're putting on a show. Let's make it happen.

Answer a few easy questions, and we'll build the structure, schedule, and forms as we go.

By the end, you'll have:
Your audition rounds mapped out A full schedule in place Time slots ready to book Forms and questions set A page you can share

We'll build this as a draft. Nothing will publish until you choose to make it live.

You can change anything later. Nothing is locked in.

Self-Tape Submissions
Let performers register and submit a self tape from a public link
▼
Registration Link
Save your audition settings to get your self-tape link.
Link available once audition is saved.
Submission Deadline (optional)
Available in emails as {{self_tape_deadline}}.
Self-Tape Brief
Shown on Step 1 of the self tape wizard and available in emails as {{self_tape_instructions}}.
Show to Performers
When on, performers can register and submit a self tape through your public link
Visible
Principal
The story depends on them. If you remove them, major parts of the show stop working or stop making sense. They are tied to the main plot, appear often, influence others, and drive decisions, conflicts, relationships, or turning points.
Supporting
The story uses them, but does not depend on them. They add clarity, texture, or emotional weight. They may appear multiple times or have a small arc, but they respond more than they initiate.
Featured
This is about impact, not size. The audience notices them clearly. A moment is built around them, they have a defined bit or purpose, and you remember them, even if the story still works without them.
Cameo
A quick, contained appearance. One moment, one scene, sometimes less. They deliver something specific and leave. No sustained arc, no thread to track, just a single clear beat.
Show on public audition page

Custom Audition Questions
The audition form starts with these standard sections. Toggle sections on or off, and add your own questions at the bottom.
Required Sections
Contact Information
First NameLast NamePhone NumberEmail Address
Always included - required for all auditions
Required
Actor Details
Preferred NamePronounsBirthdayAge (auto-calculated)
Required
Minor / Guardian Logic
Adult or Minor toggleGuardian Name, Phone, Email (shown if minor)
Required
Headshot Upload
Photo upload (optional)
Required
Optional Sections
Conflicts & Availability
Available for all rehearsals?List any conflictsAvailable for all performances?
Acting Experience
How would you describe yourself?Tell us more (optional)
Vocal Experience
How would you describe yourself?Vocal type (optional)Tell us more (optional)
Dance / Movement Experience
How would you describe yourself?Dance styles (optional)Tell us more (optional)
Special Skills
Anything that could be useful on stageTell us more (optional)
Previous Experience (optional)
Quick overview of past experienceTell us more (optional)
Casting Preference
Roles interested inHappy to be considered for any roleHappy to be considered for ensemble

Registration
Registration Dashboard
Track what every accepted cast member still needs to finish before the production is admin-ready.
Payments
Track registration fees separately from casting. Payment can be required for completion or tracked as an internal admin item.
Payment Tracker
Send the registration link
Decide whether BTS sends links automatically after a role is accepted, and set the deadline families will see.
1A
Registration Due By
The deadline shown to the performer on their registration page. Leave blank for no deadline.
Send Registration Link Automatically
When on, BTS emails the registration link as soon as a performer accepts their role.
When someone accepts
Choose what BTS should do automatically the moment a performer says yes.
1B
Choose the form sections
Tick the sections you want performers to complete. Youth sections can appear automatically based on the age rules.
2A
How the form behaves
Set what performers can edit, what staff can customize, and what locks after submission.
2B
Age brackets
Set the age cutoffs that define child, teen, and adult performers. These brackets drive which registration sections appear and who is allowed to sign forms on their own. Ages are calculated from date of birth, not a stored age field.
3A
Child Performer
Performers below this age are treated as children. Guardian and pickup rules apply.
Teen / Minor Performer
Performers in this range are minors. Guardian signature rules apply but pickup requirements depend on the child bracket.
Adult Performer
Adults can sign their own forms and do not require a guardian contact unless you override that below.
Guardian Required
Performers below this age must provide a guardian contact as part of their registration.
Can Sign Own Forms
Performers at or above this age can sign agreements themselves. Below this, a guardian must co-sign.
Pickup / Drop-off Required
Performers below this age must have approved pickup adults on file before registration is complete.
Age-triggered requirements
Choose what youth and adult performers must complete based on the brackets above.
3B
Define complete
Tell BTS what has to be finished before a cast member counts as registered.
3C
Conflict Confirmation
Whether performers must tick a box confirming they have read and accept the rehearsal schedule conflicts.
Edits After Submission
What happens when a performer tries to change something after submitting. Staff approval sends you a notification before the change goes through.
Registration Due Date
The date by which all cast members must have their registration finished. Used for reminders and completion tracking.
Required information
Choose what information blocks completion. Unticked items are still tracked, but they do not stop registration from being marked done.
3D
Set the fee
Add a fee only if you need one. If there is no fee, set the amount to zero and hide payment from the performer.
4A
Registration Fee
The amount each cast member owes. Set to 0 if there is no fee.
Payment Due Date
When payment must be received by. Shown to performers on their registration page.
Payment Status Rule
Track only: payment is recorded but does not block completion. Required: blocks the completed status. Hidden: no payment section appears.
Payment Instructions
These instructions appear on the registration form so performers and families know exactly how to pay and who to contact with questions.
Choose agreements
Pick the documents that must be signed before registration can be complete.
3E
Explain the signing step
Add one short note before the agreement section so families know what they are signing.
3F
Agreement Instructions
Shown to the performer just before they reach the documents. Keep it short and friendly.
Show the commitment
Put the rehearsal pattern and key dates in front of performers before they submit conflicts.
3G
First Rehearsal Date
The date of the very first rehearsal. Shown on the registration form so performers know when things kick off.
Rehearsal Schedule
A plain-language summary of the regular rehearsal pattern. Shown to performers before they submit their conflicts.
Key Dates
Important fixed dates like tech week, dress rehearsal, and performance nights. Performers are expected to be available for these unless there is an exceptional conflict.
Schedule acknowledgement
Require cast members to confirm they understand the rehearsal commitment before their registration can be completed.
3H
Visibility and release
Control when the cast list goes public and what information the public can see. Internal data like contact details, medical records, and payment info is always kept private.
3I
Public Cast List
Hidden keeps the cast list completely private. Draft preview lets you share a link before it is officially announced. Published makes it publicly visible.
Release Date
The date the cast list switches to Published automatically. Leave blank to publish manually.
Public Info
What appears on the public cast list. Choose the level of detail that fits your programme and performer privacy preferences.
Internal vs public data
These settings keep sensitive information away from the public cast list. Both are on by default and should only be turned off for productions with special data-sharing requirements.
3J
Turn messages on
Choose which registration messages send automatically. Turn off anything you want to handle yourself.
5A

Page
Full Link
Page
Team Portal URL
Share this link with your creative team. It takes them straight to the audition portal login.

What's on this page
Production Settings

Shape how this production appears and behaves

Keep the essentials tidy here: core production details, public link structure, location defaults, and which modules are available to the team.
Production-level controls
Details
Modules
Danger Zone

Production identity

Set the show title, poster, season, performer age range, and the short description used across your production surfaces.
Details
—

Team Portal URL

The link your creative team uses to log in to the audition portal. Read-only — derived from your organisation shortcode and show title.
Team
BuildTheShow.com/ — / — / Team

Default locations

Save the venues you use most often for auditions, rehearsals, and performances so scheduling stays consistent throughout the production.
Spaces
Audition
Rehearsal
Performance

Region defaults

Choose the holiday region used when production scheduling needs country and province context.
Region

Module visibility

Turn features on or off for this production. Disabled modules are hidden from the shared production navigation and related production views.
Modules

Keep this list intentional. Show only the areas the production is actually using right now so the workspace stays focused.

Danger Zone

Permanent actions live here. Keep them visually separate from normal production edits.
Careful

Delete this production

Permanently removes all associated data — auditions, cast lists, calendar events, and files. This cannot be undone.

Voice & Movement
Import Characters
Paste character names - one per line.
Import with AI

Character details the AI looks for:

  • Name and role type (Principal, Supporting, Featured, Ensemble, etc.)
  • Age or range (teens, 30s-40s, child)
  • Gender
  • Voice type and vocal range (if singing)
  • Dance/movement requirement
  • How many performers play this role
  • Description - shows on audition forms & public page
